mnemonics: MRS. CPU - Manages Resources & Services for CPU
keyPoints: The OS acts as a resource manager for hardware like CPU, memory, and I/O devices.
OS structures (monolithic, microkernel, layered) define how management services are organized and interact.
Key management functions include process scheduling, memory allocation, file storage, and device handling.
commonMistakes: Confusing the OS with the hardware itself; it is the software that manages the hardware.
Assuming all operating systems use the same internal structure (e.g., monolithic vs. microkernel).
Overlooking that resource management often involves trade-offs (e.g., performance vs. security, fairness vs. efficiency).
